denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 02/07/2026
UnitedHealthcare Choice Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W100118785          Group #: GRP-1015
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-106076
Claim #: CLM-2026-188796
Date of Service (requested): 01/18/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Northline Medical Group

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 01/20/2026 for Adult ADHD coaching plus medication management extension related to Attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder, combined (F90.2). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Adult ADHD coaching plus medication management extension
Diagnosis (as submitted): Attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder, combined (F90.2)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied: CDG-UM115 under UnitedHealthcare Coverage Determination Guideline (CDG) and applicable Medical Policy Bulletin

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CDG-UM115 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Coverage requires a defined course of conservative therapy before Adult ADHD coaching plus medication management extension. Documentation does not show the required duration or type of conservative care.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with conservative treatment required. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the verbal timeframe communicated to office staff (not confirmed in this letter).

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a UnitedHealthcare medical director. Peer-to-peer review must be requested before a formal appeal is initiated for the same authorization decision.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100118785) and reference UM-2026-106076. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination.

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
Clinical Operations
clinical_context: The 49-year-old member has Attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder, combined (F90.2). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Adult ADHD coaching plus medication management extension recommended as the next appropriate step. Documented workplace impairment; atomoxetine 80 mg daily trialed 10 weeks with partial response. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that conservative treatment required bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings (including dated scale scores in the chart) that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if his care is delayed further.
